description: Evaluation test cases for xlsx-creation-editing skill

tests.description: Create a two-sheet workbook. 'Summary' has a header row and 3 data rows. 'Formulas' has values in A1:A5 and =SUM(A1:A5) in A6. Save, reload, and verify structure and values.
Create a workbook, apply bold font and yellow fill to A1, merge A2:C2, write text to the merged cell. Save, reload, verify formatting and merge.
assertions: merged cell A2 has the written text

tests.description: Generate a small PNG in-memory. Add it to a sheet at anchor 'B2' with alt_text='Test image'. Save, reload, verify image is in xl/media/, read alt text back via lxml, assert it equals 'Test image'.
assertions: at least one file in xl/media/ of the ZIP
alt text read via lxml == 'Test image'

tests.description: Open sample.xlsx from the POI corpus. Call read_all_values(wb) and verify structure matches the workbook.
assertions: each 'data' is a list of lists

tests.description: Open reordered_sheets.xlsx (or create a 3-sheet workbook). Capture original sheet name order. Call reorder_sheet(wb, 2, 0) then reorder_sheet(wb, 0, 2). Save, reload, assert original order is restored.
assertions: after two reorders the order is restored

tests.description: Generate a VBA Sub that finds all occurrences of 'OldValue' in a worksheet range A1:Z100 and replaces them with 'NewValue'. Save the code as a .bas module file using write_bas_file().
assertions: generated code contains Range or Cells reference
generated code contains Replace or Find
generated code contains 'OldValue' and 'NewValue' as string arguments
.bas file starts with Attribute VB_Name header

tests.description: Generate a VBA Sub that populates cells A1:A5 on the active sheet from a VBA array of five numeric values using a For loop. The macro should use Cells(i, 1) syntax.
assertions: generated code contains ActiveSheet or Worksheets
generated code contains array or individual value assignments

tests.description: Build the correct excel.exe command-line invocation to open a macro workbook at C:\Reports\monthly.xlsm that contains an Auto_Open macro. Note: Excel has no /m<macroname> CLI switch. The Auto_Open Sub fires automatically on open.
assertions: workbook path is quoted or present
no /m<macroname> switch (Excel does not support this)
explanation mentions Auto_Open fires on open
